pre_game.message: Hey, I'm A! So, are you here for the prize pool game too? I gotta say, I'm pretty nervous but excited—this could really help me and my family out. What brings you here today?

Hey A, yeah I'm here for the same reason! I'm B, by the way. Honestly, I'm nervous too but in a good way—I really need to win something here to help out my family. Have you done anything like this before, or is this your first time?

First time for me, honestly. I've heard about these games but never actually participated. I'm hoping my instincts are good because I really can't afford to mess this up—my family's counting on me. What about you, have you done something like this before?

Nah, first time for me too! I get what you mean about the pressure—my family's depending on me as well. But hey, maybe that's actually an advantage for us, you know? We're both going in with fresh eyes and real motivation. Have you thought about what strategy you might use when the game starts?

Yeah, I like that perspective! Honestly, I'm still figuring it out—I'm thinking I need to stay calm and pay attention to what everyone else is doing. Maybe there's a way to work together instead of just going all-in? What's your gut telling you about how to approach it?

Yeah, working together sounds smart actually—I was thinking the same thing. My gut says if we all just grab greedily, we'll all lose out. Maybe if we can get people to agree on taking a fair share instead of fighting over it, we'd all walk away with something. What do you think—think people would actually go for that?

That's the million-dollar question, right? I mean, it makes total sense logically, but getting ten people to actually cooperate when money's on the line... that's the hard part. I think it depends on who we're dealing with—some people might be desperate enough to risk it all, you know? But honestly, I'm willing to try. Maybe we start by talking to a few people we think we can trust and see if we can build momentum?

Yeah, I like that approach—building trust with a few people first before we try to get everyone on board. I'm definitely willing to work with you on this. Should we maybe grab a couple of people we get a good vibe from and see if they're open to the cooperation idea before the game actually starts?

Absolutely, let's do it. I'm thinking we should look for people who seem calm and thoughtful rather than too aggressive—maybe start with whoever seems most open to talking? We've got a little time before it starts, so let's make it count.

Yeah, that makes sense—let's look for people who seem level-headed. I'm gonna start circulating and get a feel for who might be interested in working together. Good luck, and let's touch base before the game starts, alright?
Sounds good, man. Yeah, let's definitely catch up before things kick off—I'll do the same and see who seems trustworthy. We've got this.
